narrative:
Rodri is a pivotal defensive midfielder for Spain, earning an impressive overall rating of 7.45/10. This places him in the top 1% of defensive midfielders at this tournament. His rating range of 7.04–7.86 reflects a strong performance profile across his 267 World Cup minutes played.

At this World Cup, Rodri demonstrates his defensive solidity with 2.697 tackles per 90 minutes and 1.348 clearances per 90 minutes. He also contributes offensively, recording 1.348 key passes per 90 minutes and 0.191 expected assists per 90 minutes. However, his attacking output is modest, with only 0.05 expected goals and 0.674 shots per 90 minutes at this tournament.

Rodri serves as Spain's crucial anchor in midfield, with 61% of his rating from club form and 39% from this World Cup highlighting his consistent high level. Scouts should continue to observe his exceptional defensive work rate and his ability to initiate attacks through key passes. His influence on ball progression and midfield control will be vital in Spain's upcoming fixtures.
